name: Puerto del Bellotar Loop
name_original: Puerto del Bellotar por la Sierra de Calderón — circular desde Siles
description: This difficult 42.7-mile (68.8 km) road cycling route from Siles climbs 4926 feet (1501 metres) to Puerto del Bellotar in Sierra de Calderón
region_intro: Road cycling around Siles features a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, offering challenging climbs and descents. The region includes mountainous terrain with numerous passes and viewpoints, providing varied routes for road cyclists. Routes often traverse through natural parks and past historical sites, with some trails leading to high peaks and along river valleys. The area's topography makes it suitable for advanced road bike climbs Siles. Best road cycling routes around Siles The most popular road cycling route is Puerto del Bellotar Loop, a 42.8 miles (68.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 49 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain, making it ideal for advanced road cyclists. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Siles – tour around Castillo de Seguro de la Sierra, a difficult 30.4 miles (49.0 km) path. This route offers scenic views around a historic castle and includes significant climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Bienservida and Villapalacios — circular, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. This moderate route provides a balanced cycling experience. Road cycling around Siles is defined by mountain passes, river valleys, and historic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Siles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Siles?
A: There are over 30 dedicated road cycling routes around Siles, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is particularly known for its challenging terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Siles?
A: Road cycling in Siles is characterized by significant elevation changes, including challenging climbs and descents. You'll find mountainous terrain with numerous passes and viewpoints, often traversing through natural parks and past historical sites, with some routes leading to high peaks and along river valleys.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Siles?
A: While Siles is known for its challenging climbs, there are 11 moderate road cycling routes available. These routes offer a balanced cycling experience without the extreme difficulty of the advanced trails, making them more suitable for those seeking a less strenuous ride or families with older children. An example of a moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280197937/bienservida-and-villapalacios-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bienservida and Villapalacios — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Siles?
A: Siles is an excellent destination for advanced road cyclists, with 22 difficult routes. These often feature substantial elevation gain and demanding climbs. Notable challenging routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277938337/puerto-del-bellotar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Bellotar Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009259075/siles-tour-around-castillo-de-seguro-de_la_sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siles – tour around Castillo de Seguro de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Siles?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Siles are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280197937/bienservida-and-villapalacios-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bienservida and Villapalacios — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through rural landscapes.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take in Siles?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280197937/bienservida-and-villapalacios-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bienservida and Villapalacios — circular&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours 25 minutes, while more difficult and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277938337/puerto-del-bellotar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Bellotar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically require around 3 hours 49 minutes or more.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I find along road cycling routes in Siles?
A: The mountainous terrain of Siles offers numerous scenic viewpoints. Routes often pass through areas with panoramic vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hospedería Río Zumeta – Salto de la Novia Viewpoint loop from Alcantarilla de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a viewpoint. You'll also encounter stunning views from various mountain passes and peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while road cycling in Siles?
A: Yes, many routes in Siles traverse past historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009259075/siles-tour-around-castillo-de-seguro-de_la_sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siles – tour around Castillo de Seguro de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers scenic views around a historic castle. The region's rich history is often reflected in its architecture and natural monuments.

Q: Can I find waterfalls or natural monuments along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely! Siles is home to beautiful natural attractions. While cycling, you might be able to visit stunning waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saltaor Waterfall (Zurriero)&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4820919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Tejo Spring&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural monument.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Siles?
A: Given the significant elevation changes and mountainous terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Siles, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in lower elevations, while winters may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher altitudes, making some routes more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to mountain peaks in Siles?
A: Yes, for those seeking the challenge of summiting peaks, Siles offers routes that ascend to impressive heights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura de la Sierra – El Yelmo Peak loop from Siles&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that takes you towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo Summit (1,808 m)&lt;/a&gt;, providing a rewarding experience with panoramic views.

Q: What makes road cycling in Siles unique compared to other regions?
A: Road cycling in Siles stands out due to its dramatic and varied landscape, characterized by a high concentration of challenging mountain passes and deep river valleys. The region offers a unique blend of demanding climbs, historical sites, and natural parks, providing a diverse and rewarding experience for cyclists who appreciate significant elevation changes and stunning natural beauty.
tips: Beautiful mountain pass located after the town of Villaverde de Guadalimar. It is demanding and long. Now, it is worth getting to the top and enjoying the views.
Beautiful and elevated road that allows you to see the set of landscapes that lie at the foot of the valleys. The road winds from one side to the other!
The town of Siles is located in the Guadalimar river valley, in the Sierra de la Segura. The town skyline is dominated by El Cubo, a medieval defensive structure left over from a 13th century castle.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Puerto del Bellotar circular route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route usually takes around 3 hours and 50 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and demands a very good level of physical fitness. The ascent to Puerto del Bellotar is exacting and long, making it more suitable for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Where does the Puerto del Bellotar circular route start and end?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, beginning and ending in the town of Siles. You can typically find parking within Siles to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features firm, paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. You can expect a smooth riding experience for the most part.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful and varied scenery. The Puerto del Bellotar itself offers nice mountain pass views. More broadly, the Sierra de Calderón is renowned for its colorful, changing landscapes, pine forests, and stunning vistas.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the most significant feature is the climb to Puerto del Bellotar, a key mountain pass. You'll also pass by the Road to Siles and can see the El Cubo Tower in Siles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, the Sierra de Calderón region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder than the summer heat. These seasons also often bring vibrant landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this road cycling route shares sections with several other paths, including CIMA AB03 El Bellotar, Siles - Villarrodrigo, El Padrón, and Cordel de Andalucía, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto del Bellotar route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling on paved surfaces, which is generally not suitable for dogs. It's best to leave your canine companions at home for this specific cycling adventure.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle in Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Are there any places to get food or drinks in Siles after the ride?
faqs.answer: Siles, being the start and end point of this circular route, offers options for refreshments. You can find cafes, restaurants, and pubs in the town to refuel after your challenging ride.
